Value is 0 when EULS is not used. 12493290 CB014965 prbMinEuls Minimum PRBs required by UE 12505429 CB014965 prbMaxEuls maximum number of PRBs expected by UE 12533720 CB014965 prbNmcsEuls maximum number of PRBs that can be used by UE u to attain a PHR of 0dB 12533667 CB014965 sortMetric EULS Round-2 sort metric calculated as (PRBumax,R2 / Yu) 12533660 CB014965 (ID: 12678398)

#### 2.12.2.1.137 OptimisedPuschPrbData

##### 2.12.2.1.137.0-1 OptimisedPuschPrbData OptimisedPuschPrbData: Field Description Reference First Feature [CB013204-A-UPD003]  eventHeader  [End CB013204-A-UPD003] system frame number on air,  Slot on air, Ue identifier, Physical Cell ID 11177364 CB013204-A-UPD003 [CB013204-A-UPD003]  rrmMinPuschPrb  [End CB013204-A-UPD003] If NRCELL.actOptPuschPrbAlloc is true, this parameter defines the minimum number of PRBs allowed for initial PUSCH transmission( 11199466: Minimal PRBs for PUSCH initial transmission with given MCS ).  If NRCELL.actOptPuschPrbAlloc is false or for PUSCH retransmissions, this parameter is set to 274.  default value :274   range :[0...274 ], 274 - used to indicate that this field is not used. 11199466: Minimal PRBs for PUSCH initial transmission with given MCS CB013204-A-UPD003 [CB013204-A-UPD003]  rrmMinPuschPrbAtb  [End CB013204-A-UPD003] minimal PRB computed according to ( 11199466: Minimal PRBs for PUSCH initial transmission with given MCS )with MCS0 when MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/actOptPuschPrbAlloc is set to true and rdOptPuschPrbAllocATB ( 11139156: rdOptPuschPrbAllocATB ) is set to true and qam64LowSE table is not used.  For all other cases, this parameter is set to 274.     default value : 274  range :[0...274] , 274 - used to indicate that this field is not used 11199466: Minimal PRBs for PUSCH initial transmission with given MCS 11139156: rdOptPuschPrbAllocATB CB013204-A-UPD003 [CB013204-A-UPD003]  atbStatus  [End CB013204-A-UPD003] This parameter indicates Adaptive Transmission Bandwidth (ATB) status for Basic Reliability scenario and Initial transmissions only.  Enum  {    NOTAPPLIED = 0     // if  ATB procedure is not applied.    APPLIED          =  1     //  if ATB procedure is applied and requirement is 11132110: Optimized PUSCH PRB allocation for ATB initial Tx not met    APPLIEDFORMINPRB  = 2   //  if ATB procedure is applied and requirement is 11132110: Optimized PUSCH PRB allocation for ATB initial Tx met  } 11132110: Optimized PUSCH PRB allocation for ATB initial Tx CB013204-A-UPD003 (ID: 12726551)

#### 2.12.2.1.138 dlFdPolite

##### 2.12.2.1.138.0-1 dlFdPolite After 5G-L2-PS activates FD-POLITE PRB widening (see 12533182 ) in a slot, for each UE in the subcell with allocation modified by FD-POLITE loop at least once (see 12533165 ), the information defined in the following structure shall be traced: dlFdPolite record entry: Field Description Reference First Feature eventHeader SFN, slot, Cell Identifier, UE identifier 11201048 CB013100-A startPrbBeforePoltie start PRB before starting rounds of attempts of FD-POLITE PRB widening 12533165 CB013100-A numOfPrbBeforePolite Number of PRBs before rounds of attempts of FD-POLITE PRB widening 12533165 CB013100-A mcsBeforePolite MCS before rounds of attempts of FD-POLITE PRB widening 12533165 CB013100-A tbSizeBeforePolite TBS before rounds of attempts of FD-POLITE PRB widening 12533165 CB013100-A (ID: 12737340)

#### 2.12.2.1.139 dlSsbInterferenceMitigation

##### 2.12.2.1.139.0-1 dlSsbInterferenceMitigation When SSB interference mitigation is enabled, This trace is recorded per 10×rdPeriodForSsbIm (rdPeriodForSsbIM, refer to 12731387: rdPrbNumForSsbIM ) in every candidate slots (refer to 12783505 ) . 2. This TTI EVENT should also be reported at the start of the first BLER Counter Statistic period (next slot 0) after CellSetup or CellReconfiguration. dlSsbInterferenceMitigation record entry: Field Description Reference First Feature eventHeader SFN, slot, Cell Identifier, UE identifier 11201048 CB012623-A isPrbMuting Indicate SSB IM PRB muting is enable or not in slot configured by NIDD.slotForSsbIM(except SSB slot of current cell), with the period of rdPeriodForSsbIM.  The muted PRB range is (startPrb=NIDD.cRbStartForSsbIm, numOfPrb=rdPrbNumForSsbIM).    Refer to 12731387 for rdPrbNumForSsbIM and 12731398 for rdPrbNumForSsbIM.  bool.  Note: only this information shall be available in this event if it is True. 12745472 CB012623-A numOfUnSuccessDLTransmission Number of unsuccess PDSCH transmission in configured slotForSsbIm of the SSB IM statistical period. Include NACK/DTX PDSCH transmission based on DCI11 and DCI10(msg2, msg4, transient active) in current slot. refer to 12709975 U32. 12745472 CB012623-A numOfTotalDLTransmission Number of total PDSCH transmission in configured slotForSsbIm of the SSB IM statistical period. Include PDSCH transmission based on DCI11 and DCI10(msg2, msg4, transient active). refer to 12709975 U32.  Note, Only PDSCH transmissions with HARQ feedback received by L2PS are counted. 12745472 CB012623-A bler The average BLER in configured slotForSsbIm of the SSB IM statistical period, calculated as follows:  bler = numOfUnSuccessDLTransmission / numOfTotalDLTransmission  float32, range 0 to 1 , unit is %.  Note: this value in dlSsbInterferenceMitigation will be reported in the slot where the BLER calculation was performed (refer to 12709975 ) in other cases 255.0f (like cell setup/cell reconfiguration etc) 12745472 CB012623-A prohibitTimerStatus Indicate the status of running prohibitTimer  Bool 12745472 CB012623-A (ID: 12783506)

#### 2.12.2.1.140 dlSchSlotCellInfo

##### 2.12.2.1.140.0-1 dlSchSlotCellInfo This trace record the DL per scheduling slot's cell level info. When SSB interference mitigation is enabled dlSchSlotCellInfo record entry: Field Range Description Reference First Feature eventHeader 13251348 SFN, slot, Cell Identifier, UE identifier 11201048 CB012623-A numPrbMutedSsbIM (0...128) step size:1 Indicate the number of SSB IM PRB mutingFor   RAT0 it will be multiple of RBG size    For RAT1 it will be &lt;= rdPrbNumForSsbIM (because we cannot mute common channel or blanked prb) 12707696 CB012623-A availablePrbForFdm (0...253),step size: 1, for RAT1  (0...241) step size: 16 for RAT0 Cell available PRB for UE FDM scheduling, following used PRB preempted before FDM UE selection are excluded from this available PRB: SIB, Paging, SSB, TRS holes, blanked PRB, muted PRB, etc.   Note: BbPooling limited PRB is not considered. 12533165 CB012623-A (ID: 12783571)

#### 2.12.2.1.141 ulFdBeamData

##### 2.12.2.1.141.0-2 ulFdBeamData This event shall record the UE's PUSCH beam information for TDD [CB008577-C] or FDD [End CB008577-D] beamforming when a UE is scheduled in UL FD and either of the following conditions is met: The selected PUSCH patternId is changed, or The interval since the previous UL FD scheduling is equal to or greater than rdTtitraceRecordInterval This event shall be sent only when actBeamforming = TRUE. Field Range Description Reference First feature EventHeader structure SFN on air, Slot on air, Physical Cell ID 11201048 subcellId (0.. ulSubcellIdMax ), step size: 1 Ul Subcell identifier.  Same as ulSubcellId in ulSubcellList structure of PsCell_CellSetupReq message 10968024 patternId1 (0..2^16-1), step size: 1 1st pattern ID.  For 7-2a:    - For CPRI, set to 0.    - For eCPRI, same as eCpriConfig.patternId [0] of UlData_PuschReceiveReq message.  For 7-2e:    - same as patternId[0] of UlData_PuschReceiveReqL1ru message. 11513756 11513986 11513820 5GC001086-C patternId2 (0..2^16-1), step size: 1 2nd pattern ID.  For 7-2a:    - For CPRI, set to 0.   - For eCPRI, same as eCpriConfig.patternId [1] of UlData_PuschReceiveReq message.  For 7-2e:    - same as patternId[1] of UlData_PuschReceiveReqL1ru message if exist, otherwise set as 0. 11513756 11513986 11513820 5GC001086-C patternId3 (0..2^16-1), step size: 1 3rd pattern ID.  For 7-2a:    - For CPRI, set to 0.   - For eCPRI, same as eCpriConfig.patternId [2] of UlData_PuschReceiveReq message. For 7-2e:    - same as patternId[2] of UlData_PuschReceiveReqL1ru message if exist, otherwise set as 0. 11513756 11513986 11513820 CB009363-A patternId4 (0..2^16-1), step size: 1 4th pattern ID.  For 7-2a:    - For CPRI, set to 0.   - For eCPRI, same as eCpriConfig.patternId [3] of UlData_PuschReceiveReq message. For 7-2e:    - same as patternId[3] of UlData_PuschReceiveReqL1ru message if exist, otherwise set as 0. 11513756 11513986 11513820 CB009363-A patternId5 (0..2^16-1), step size: 1 5th pattern ID.  For 7-2a:    - set to 0.  For 7-2e:    - same as patternId[4] of UlData_PuschReceiveReqL1ru message if exist, otherwise set as 0. 11513756 11513986 11513820 CB010708-D patternId6 (0..2^16-1), step size: 1 6th pattern ID.  For 7-2a:    - set to 0.  For 7-2e:    - same as patternId[5] of UlData_PuschReceiveReqL1ru message if exist, otherwise set as 0. 11513756 11513986 11513820 CB010708-D patternId7 (0..2^16-1), step size: 1 7th pattern ID.  For 7-2a:    - set to 0.  For 7-2e:    - same as patternId[6] of UlData_PuschReceiveReqL1ru message if exist, otherwise set as 0. 11513756 11513986 11513820 CB010708-D patternId8 (0..2^16-1), step size: 1 8th pattern ID.  For 7-2a:    - set to 0.  For 7-2e:    - same as patternId[7] of UlData_PuschReceiveReqL1ru message if exist, otherwise set as 0. 11513756 11513986 11513820 CB010708-D isUl2ndBeamIdNeighborSelected TRUE, FALSE Indicate if second beamId in UE context is random neighbor selected. Used in 4RX or 8RX 11112298 CB009363-E (ID: 13340072)

#### 2.12.2.1.142 dlEirpAllowanceSlotData

##### 2.12.2.1.142.0-1 dlEirpAllowanceSlotData This trace records the DL EIRP EPIC Allowance related information, per slot. The event shall be traced whenever actEpic flag (received in PsCell_CellSetupReq::eirpControlConfig ( 10969144 ) and PsCell_CellReconfigurationReq::eirpControlConfig ( 10971382 )) is TRUE. dlEirpAllowanceSlotData record entry: Field Range Description Reference First feature eventHeader structure SFN, slot, Cell Identifier, UE identifier. 11201048 CB013505-A currentSlotInPeriod (0..79), step size: 1 Slot. 12526294 CB013505-A segments array [0..59] of dlEirpAllowanceSlotSegment Slot allowance information. 12526294 CB013505-A Where dlEirpAllowanceSlotSegment is defined as: Field Range Description Reference First feature allowance floating point Token allowance (unit: EirpToken = dB * PRB). 12526294 CB013505-A availableTokensInCurrentSlot floating point Number of tokens avaliable in current slot (unit: EirpToken = dB * PRB). 12526294 CB013505-A consumedTokensInPreviousSlots floating point Number of tokens consumed in the current measurement period (unit: EirpToken = dB * PRB). 12526294 CB013505-A cumulativeMinimalTokenAllocation floating point Cumulative number of tokens contribution from the rrmMinEirpTokenPerPeriod (unit: EirpToken = dB * PRB). 12526294 CB013505-A segmentId (0..59), step size: 1 Cell segment index 12526294 CB013505-A (ID: 13255835)

#### 2.12.2.1.143 dlEirpAllowancePeriodData

##### 2.12.2.1.143.0-1 dlEirpAllowancePeriodData This trace records the DL EIRP EPIC Allowance related information, per period (by default, period equals 10 SFNs). The event shall be traced whenever actEpic flag (received in PsCell_CellSetupReq::eirpControlConfig ( 10969144 ) and PsCell_CellReconfigurationReq::eirpControlConfig ( 13255835 )) is TRUE. dlEirpAllowancePeriodData record entry: Field Range Description Reference First feature eventHeader structure SFN, slot, Cell Identifier, UE identifier. 11201048 CB013505-A segments array [0..59] of dlEirpAllowancePeriodSegment Period allowance information. 12526294 CB013505-A Where dlEirpAllowancePeriodSegment is defined as: Field Range Description Reference First feature rrmEirpControlTokenInTheBucket floating point Number of tokens available in the current measurement period (unit: EirpToken = dB * PRB). 12526294 CB013505-A consumedTokensInPreviousPeriod floating point Number of tokens consumed in the previous measurement period (unit: EirpToken = dB * PRB). 12526294 CB013505-A eirpMaxUsageCapacityPerSlot floating point Maximum EIRP that would be used in the current period for slot if all RBGs are allocated to the current segment at full power (unit: EirpToken = dB * PRB). 12526294 CB013505-A segmentId (0..59), step size: 1 Cell segment index. 12526294 CB013505-A (ID: 13255862)

#### 2.12.2.1.144 processingDelayInfo

##### 2.12.2.1.144.0-1 processingDelayInfo (ID: 13396912)

**2.12.2.1.144.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13360658`)

processingDelayInfo This TTI Trace records crucial moments in Delay state machine [11170529: Delay State machine](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26K3QPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) which handle processing delay for RRC Reconfiguration.

The event can be traced when FAF: actRrcReconfProcessingDelay is set to TRUE ([10967202: PsCell_CellSetupReq Message Content](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTdPu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) and Profil 0 (Default) or Profil 1 (All events) or Profil 12 (UE blocking) is selected for collecting TTI trace logs

TTI trace event is triggered for collecting when:

  * in sunny day scenario, when RrcProcessingDelayTimer has expired or,
  * in rainy day scenario, when:

  * harqRrcMsgTimeoutTimer expires, or,

  * reRACH is triggered, or,
  * 5G-L2-PS receives RRCReconfigCompletedReq message from L3 before RrcProcessingDelayTimer expiration.



processingDelayInfo record entry:

|  |  |  |   
---|---|---|---|---  
Field | Range | Description | Reference | First feature  
eventHeader | [structure](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29tTf_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) | SFN, slot, Cell Identifier, UE identifier. | [11201048](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29tTfvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) | CNI-161127  
macLayerMonitoringStart | structure | SFN and slot value received by 5G-L2-PS in LoCtrl_DlBufferStatusInd message. (see transition B in Delay State Machine) | [11170529: Delay State machine](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26K3QPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) | CNI-161127  
>sfn | (0..1023, step size:1) |  |  |   
>slot | (0..79, step size:1) |  |  |   
firstFragment | structure | SFN and slot value when first fragment of RRC Reconfiguration is sent by 5G-L2-PS (see transition C in Delay State Machine) | [11170529: Delay State machine](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26K3QPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) | CNI-161127  
>sfn | (0..1023, step size:1) |  |  |   
>slot | (0..79, step size:1) |  |  |   
rrcDelayStart | structure | SFN and slot value when RrcProcessingDelayTimer is started by 5G-L2-PS (see transition E in Delay State Machine) | [11170529: Delay State machine](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26K3QPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) | CNI-161127  
>sfn | (0..1023, step size:1) |  |  |   
>slot | (0..79, step size:1) |  |  |   
rrcDelayStop | structure | SFN and slot value when RrcProcessingDelayTimer expires in 5G-L2-PS. | [11170529: Delay State machine](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26K3QPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) | CNI-161127  
>sfn | (0..1023, step size:1) |  |  |   
>slot | (0..79, step size:1) |  |  |   
macLayerMonitoringStop | structure | SFN and slot value when harqRrcMsgTimeoutTimer: * in sunny day scenario, when it would expire, * in rainy day scenario, when it expires. | [11170529: Delay State machine](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_26K3QPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) | CNI-161127  
>sfn | (0..1023, step size:1) |  |  |   
>slot | (0..79, step size:1) |  |  |   
  
Note1: Standard 5G NR radio frame has 10ms duration. Number of slots within this frame depend on subcarrier spacing (SCS):

  * In FDD FR1 (SCS=15kHz) 10ms radio frame contains 10 slots, which are numbered 0 to 9
  * In TDD FR1 (SCS=30kHz) 10ms radio frame contains 20 slots, which are numbered 0 to 19
  * in TDD FR2 (SCS=120kHz) 10ms radio frame contains 80 slots, which are numbered 0 to 79

#### 2.12.2.1.145 dlEirpEpicSegmentData

##### 2.12.2.1.145.0-1 dlEirpEpicSegmentData This trace records the DL EIRP EPIC cell segment related information, per EPIC slot. The event shall be traced whenever actEpic flag (received in PsCell_CellSetupReq::eirpControlConfig ( 10969144 ) and PsCell_CellReconfigurationReq::eirpControlConfig ( 10971382 )) is TRUE. dlEirpEpicSegmentData record entry: Field Range Description Reference First feature eventHeader structure SFN, slot, Cell Identifier, UE identifier. 11201048 CB013505-A segments array [0..59] of dlEirpEpicSegment EPIC per segment information. 12533541 CB013505-A Where dlEirpEpicSegment is defined as: Field Range Description Reference First feature vMin floating point Lower limit of the Lagrangian multiplier in the EPIC power and MCS reduction algorithm. 12533541 CB013505-A vMax floating point Upper limit of the Lagrangian multiplier in the EPIC power and MCS reduction algorithm. 12533541 CB013505-A vOptimal floating point Result of the outer dichotomy search in EPIC power and MCS reduction algorithm. 12533541 CB013505-A tokensPerSegmentPerSubcell floating point Tokens available in a given segment for a given subcell.  (unit: EirpToken = dB * PRB). 12533541 CB013505-A segmentId (0..59), step size: 1 Cell segment index 12533541 CB013505-A (ID: 13369368)

#### 2.12.2.1.146 dlEirpEpicUeData

##### 2.12.2.1.146.0-1 dlEirpEpicUeData This trace records the DL EIRP EPIC UE related information, per UE, per EPIC slot. The event shall be traced whenever actEpic flag (received in PsCell_CellSetupReq::eirpControlConfig ( 10969144 ) and PsCell_CellReconfigurationReq::eirpControlConfig ( 10971382 )) is TRUE. DlEirpEpicUeData record entry: Field Range Description Reference First feature eventHeader structure SFN, slot, Cell Identifier, UE identifier. 11201048 CB013505-A powerMax floating point Max power used in EPIC power and MCS reduction algorithm. Final UE power calculated by EPIC will not be greater than powerMax. (unit: dB). 12533541 CB013505-A powerMin floating point Min power used in EPIC power and MCS reduction algorithm. Final UE power calculated by EPIC will not be lower than powerMin.  (unit: dB). 12533541 CB013505-A noisePower floating point Input for EPIC power and MCS reduction algorithm. It is calculated from UE data and PDCCH power  (unit: dB). 12533541 CB013505-A latestSinr floating point Input for EPIC power and MCS reduction algorithm. It is taken from UE data.  (unit: dB). 12533541 CB013505-A mcsBefore (0..32), step size:1 MCS input for EPIC power and MCS reduction algorithm. Final MCS (mcsAfter) from EPIC algorithm will not be higher than mcsBefore. 12533541 CB013505-A mcsAfter (0..32), step size:1 MCS output from EPIC power and MCS reduction algorithm. Range: [epicMcsThreshold, mcsBefore] 12533541 CB013505-A firstBeamId range: (0..2720, 4095), step size: 1 Most suitable beam selected for the UE. Type: uint16. Invalid value (invalid beam Id): 4095. 12533541 PR874541 secondBeamId range: (0..2720, 4095), step size: 1 Second-best beam selected for the UE. Type: uint16. Invalid value (invalid beam Id): 4095. 12533541 PR874541 segments array [0..59] of  dlEirpEpicUeSegment EPIC UE per segment information. 12533541 CB013505-A Where dlEirpEpicUeSegment  is defined as: Field Range Description Reference First feature normalizedBeamGain floating point Input for EPIC power and MCS reduction algorithm. It is calculated from EIRP info and beam data. (unit: dB). 12533541 CB013505-A segmentId (0..59), step size: 1 Cell segment index. 12533541 CB013505-A (ID: 13369378)

The event shall be traced whenever partialZABAllowed is TRUE (received in ps::cnfg::AddressDistributionReq_t::l2RtPoolDeploymentInformations ( 10966124 ) Struct partialZabCellPrbData as below. Field Range Description Reference First feature partialZABAllocRimRs Type: uint16  Range: 0..384 Rim-Rs step allocation output in the Partial ZAB algorithm. 12804289 CB014543-H partialZABAllocPrbRound1 Type: uint16  Range: 0..4368 (273 x 16 streams) Round-1 step allocation output in the Partial ZAB algorithm. 12804289 CB014543-G/H partialZABAllocPrbRound2 Type: uint16  Range: 0..4368 (273 x 16 streams) Round-2 step allocation output in the Partial ZAB algorithm 12804289 CB014543-G/H partialZABAllocRemainingPrb Type: uint16  Range: 0..4368 (273 x 16 streams) Remaining PRB allocation output in the Partial ZAB algorithm 12804289 CB014543-G/H (ID: 13375143)

#### 2.12.2.1.148 dlMuMlUeTrace

##### 2.12.2.1.148.0-1 dlMuMlUeTrace This trace records the ML based massive MIMO DL MU pairing (CB015912, see 13375160) related information for ML training purposes, in period of once per slot. dlMuMlUeTrace record entry: Field Range Description Reference First feature eventHeader structure SFN, slot, Cell Identifier, UE identifier. 11201048 CB015912-A dnnBeamIndex (0…48), step size: 1 Indicates UE DNN Beam Index for ML based massive MIMO DL MU pairing. 13375160 CB015912-A virtualUeIndex (0…31), step size: 1 Index of the virtual UE created in the specified slot.  31 for UEs that are not paired in slot.  30 for UEs that are included to DNN SumMuPf calculation but are not included to first ML vUE. 11142531 CB015912-A legacyUePf Floating point (0…2000) UE’s PF value by using MU-SINR instead of SU-SINR calculated for legacy virtual UE. 13375160 CB015912-A uePf Floating point (0…2000) UE’s PF value by using MU-SINR instead of SU-SINR. 13375160 CB015912-A rootUeRnti (1...2^16-1), step size: 1 rnti of the root UE of the specified virtual UE. 11152029 CB015912-A legacySumMuPF Floating point (0…12000) The summary of BeamPF for the first virtual UE construct by legacy method. 13375160 CB015912-A dnnSumMuPF Floating point (0…12000) The summary of BeamPF for DNN selected beams by MU algorithm. 13375160 CB015912-A isBeamSelectedByAIML (0…1, 2), step size: 1 Indicates if the UE pairing in the current TTI is by AI/ML scheme or by legacy scheme.  If this UE is paired by AI/ML scheme, then the value is set to 1; otherwise set to 0.  For non-paired UE value is set to 2. 13375160 CB015912-A rrmCorrAvgCqi Floating point The corrected average CQI as calculated in 11125689 . Differently than the homonymous parameter in 11125689 , it includes also the CQI corrections in TRS/CSI-IM/special- slots. 11125689 CB015912-A avgTput Floating point Average UE Data rate. CB015912-A numRequestedRBsAtSuCQI (0…273), step size: 1 Indicates the initial request PRB number for this UE according to UE’s buffer. When calculate this value, assume UE is SU without pairing. CB015912-A mcsTable Enum{  qam64,  qam256,  qam64LowSE,  qam1024,  cancelMcsTableReconfiguration} Enum{  qam64 = 0,  qam256 = 1,  qam64LowSE = 2,  qam1024 = 3,  cancelMcsTableReconfiguration = 4} 11178401 CB015912-A rank Enum{  RANK1, RANK2, RANK3, RANK4, RANK5, RANK6, RANK7, INVALID_RANK} UE rank indicator.  Enum{ RANK1 = 1, RANK2 = 2, RANK3 = 3, RANK4 = 4, RANK5 = 5, RANK6 = 6, RANK7 = 7, INVALID_RANK = 15} 11164084 CB015912-A retxInitialTbs (0..2^32-1), step size:1 Transport block size. 11203355 CB015912-A deltaCqi Floating point Delta CQI value. 11129961 CB015912-A mlWeight Floating point (0…1) ML UE weight for AI/ML algorithms. 13375160 CB015912-A txNumber (0...maxUlHarqTxNum), step size:1 1 = fresh transmission, 1 &gt; for retransmissions.   For maxUlHarqTxNum (Maximum number of UL HARQ transmssions), see 11203324 11201619 CB015912-A (ID: 13401995)

##### 2.12.2.1.150-1.0-6 Simultaneous PUCCH and PUSCH Transmission dciDynamicParamsLength (ID: 13482416)

**2.12.2.1.150-1.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `13482424`)

Simultaneous PUCCH and PUSCH Transmission This parameter is bit field of lenght 32 bits. Within each range of bits (denoted as bits position) is encoded length of DCI field with dynamic (variable) length (in bits). Not present fields will have length 0. Example: fdResourceAssignment field with number of bits 5 can suport values from 0 to 31 so that fdResourceAssignment in DCI can occupy 0 to 31 bits (in practice more narrow range is supported by system). Dependent on DCI format (see: [13457381](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_3vUw0y20EfGxldjOZQLezQ)) different set of dynamic fields is supported:

For DCI Format 0_0:

|  |   
---|---|---  
dynamic length field name | number of bits | bits position  
fdResourceAssignment | 5 | 0..4  
paddingBits | 5 | 5..9  
reserved | 18 | 10..27  
version | 4 | 28..31  
  
For DCI Format 0_1:

|  |   
---|---|---  
dynamic length field name | number of bits | bits position  
bwPartIndicator | 2 | 0..1  
fdResourceAssignment | 5 | 2..6  
tdResourceAssignment | 3 | 7..9  
precodingInfAndNumOfLayers | 3 | 10..12  
antennaPort | 3 | 13..15  
csiRequest | 1 | 16  
ptrsDmrs | 2 | 17..18  
betaOffsetIndicator | 2 | 19..20  
dmrsSequenceInitialization | 1 | 21  
pdcchMonitoringAdaptationIndication | 1 | 22  
reserved | 5 | 23..27  
version | 4 | 28..31  
  
For DCI Format 1_0:

|  |   
---|---|---  
dynamic length field name | number of bits | bits position  
fdResourceAssignment | 5 | 0..4  
reserved | 23 | 5..27  
version | 4 | 28..31  
  
For DCI Format 1_1:

|  |   
---|---|---  
dynamic length field name | number of bits | bits position  
bwPartIndicator | 2 | 0..1  
fdResourceAssignment | 5 | 2..6  
tdResourceAssignment | 3 | 7..9  
downlinkAssigmentIndex | 3 | 10..12  
antennaPort | 3 | 13..15  
pdcchMonitoringAdaptationIndication | 1 | 16  
reserved | 11 | 17..27  
version | 4 | 28..31  
  
Note:

  1. Above tables describe only DCI fields with dynamic length. Other fixed size fields are described in [11171389](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_xTxeM_vNEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) or TS 38.212 chapter 7.3.1.1.
  2. Version is not describing any DCI field and is only used to indicate current version of trace. Currently it has value of "2" and shall be incremented in case update to above tables is made.

---

##### 2.12.2.1.150.0-1 Simultaneous PUCCH and PUSCH Transmission SimulPucchPuschTx (ID: 13461483)

**2.12.2.1.150.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `13461484`)

Simultaneous PUCCH and PUSCH Transmission Traced when there is change in the parallelTxForUlCA sent from Pcell to Scell after the CB014871 related power calculations([12882347](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ijfnALR4EfCQT7hYUxERAg?oslc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_5KBG5p5DEe2A-5U7tzedRA)) at Pcell.

SimulPucchPuschTx entry:

|  |  |   
---|---|---|---  
**Field** | **Description** | **Reference** | **First Feature**  
EventHeader | UE selection SFN on air, Slot on air, no Ue identifier, Physical Cell ID | [12882347](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ijfnALR4EfCQT7hYUxERAg?oslc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_5KBG5p5DEe2A-5U7tzedRA) | CB014871-A  
UeParallelTxInfo | A dynamic array with max size of MAX_NUM_OF_ACTIVE_FR1_CA_UE, include UEs that are selected for paralle TX. | [12882347](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ijfnALR4EfCQT7hYUxERAg?oslc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_5KBG5p5DEe2A-5U7tzedRA) | CB014871-A  
>rnti | Ue identifier: C-RNTI | [12882347](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ijfnALR4EfCQT7hYUxERAg?oslc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_5KBG5p5DEe2A-5U7tzedRA) | CB014871-A  
>isParallelTx | Enum { DISABLED = 0 ENABLED = 1 } | Traced when power equations at PCELL for a UE is satisfied([12882347](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ijfnALR4EfCQT7hYUxERAg?oslc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_5KBG5p5DEe2A-5U7tzedRA)), parallelTxForUlCA flag is sent to Scell and UE scheduled on Scell(FDD). | CB014871-A

### 2.12.2.2 Trace bitrate control

### 2.12.2.2-1 Trace bitrate control

### 2.12.2.3 Trace filtering

#### 2.12.2.3.0-1 Trace filtering Control structure (ID: 11206851)

**2.12.2.3.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11206880`)

Trace filtering 5G-L2-PS shall maintain single bitset called as: selectedComponentTracesEvents

Number of bit should be equal to the number of supported traces in 5G_L2_1601_replaced([11200764](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29ssaPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Position in selectedComponentTracesEvents is equal to Event's discriminator in 5G_L2_1601_replaced([11200764](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_29ssaPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[Before CNI-107269]

5G-L2-PS shall update selectedComponentTracesEvents to keep all information up to date which events' type are active including mandatory and optional events after each update of R&D parameters:

rdComponentBitMapVector0 (See 5G_L2_16967_replaced([11188990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28MQjPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector1 (See 5G_L2_16971_replaced([11189001](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28MQjvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector2 (See 5G_L2_16975_replaced([11189013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3kfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector3 (See 5G_L2_16977_replaced([11189022](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3k_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector4 (See 5G_L2_16979_replaced([11189033](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3lfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector5 (See 5G_L2_16981_replaced([11189044](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3l_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a table with a header row and data rows. The header row has six columns, each with a rotated label. The first column of the header is empty. The subsequent columns are labeled "rdComponentBitMapVector5", "rdComponentBitMapVector4", "rdComponentBitMapVector3", "rdComponentBitMapVector2", "rdComponentBitMapVector1", and "rdComponentBitMapVector0". The first column of the data rows contains the label "R&D param to Event's ID". The subsequent columns contain pairs of numbers and text. Here is the table represented in markdown:

| rdComponentBitMapVector5 | rdComponentBitMapVector4 | rdComponentBitMapVector3 | rdComponentBitMapVector2 | rdComponentBitMapVector1 | rdComponentBitMapVector0  
---|---|---|---|---|---|---  
R&D param to Event's ID | 191 Stop | 160 Start | 159 Stop | 128 Start | 127 Stop | 96 Start  
  
5G-L2-PS before each writing attempt of Component Trace Event to buffer shall check the appropriate bit inside selectedComponentTracesEvents.

File: rndParamMappingToEventId.jpg

Folder:[https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?cid=7fc03469%2D2b28%2D4e8f%2D9b01%2Dc87d21e45fd0&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level%2F2%205G%2DL2%2DPS%20Component%20Requirements%2F2%2E11%20Traceability%2F2%2E11%2E2%20L2%20trace%2F2%2E11%2E2%2E3%20Trace%20filtering&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?cid=7fc03469%2D2b28%2D4e8f%2D9b01%2Dc87d21e45fd0&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level%2F2%205G%2DL2%2DPS%20Component%20Requirements%2F2%2E11%20Traceability%2F2%2E11%2E2%20L2%20trace%2F2%2E11%2E2%2E3%20Trace%20filtering&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c)

[CNI-107269]

After gNB startup:

5G-L2-PS shall update selectedComponentTracesEvents to keep all information up to date which events' type are active including mandatory and optional events and selected profiles based on current values of R&D parameters:

rdComponentBitMapVector0 (See 5G_L2_16967_replaced([11188990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28MQjPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector1 (See 5G_L2_16971_replaced([11189001](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28MQjvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector2 (See 5G_L2_16975_replaced([11189013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3kfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector3 (See 5G_L2_16977_replaced([11189022](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3k_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector4 (See 5G_L2_16979_replaced([11189033](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3lfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector5 (See 5G_L2_16981_replaced([11189044](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3l_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapProfile (See 5G_L2_17107_replaced([11189055](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3mfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

---|---|---|---|---|---|---

File: rndParamMappingToEventId.jpg

Folder:[https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?cid=7fc03469%2D2b28%2D4e8f%2D9b01%2Dc87d21e45fd0&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level%2F2%205G%2DL2%2DPS%20Component%20Requirements%2F2%2E11%20Traceability%2F2%2E11%2E2%20L2%20trace%2F2%2E11%2E2%2E3%20Trace%20filtering&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Forms/Tree_View.aspx?cid=7fc03469%2D2b28%2D4e8f%2D9b01%2Dc87d21e45fd0&FolderCTID=0x012000F0A0A3BA325F7648AD307E6959BA7376&id=%2Fsites%2F5Gdoc%2FAS%5FTech%5FSpec%2FDrawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G%2FUser%20Plane%2F5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level%2F2%205G%2DL2%2DPS%20Component%20Requirements%2F2%2E11%20Traceability%2F2%2E11%2E2%20L2%20trace%2F2%2E11%2E2%2E3%20Trace%20filtering&viewid=967508b3%2D8e81%2D461c%2Db99d%2D145065cf0c1c)

After receiving request L2LogDu_StreamActivationReq:

5G-L2-PS shall update selectedComponentTracesEvents depending on the parameter profileBitMap in trace start request L2LogDu_StreamActivationReq (See 5G_L2_IF_1848_replaced([10994279](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2XDsjvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If profileBitMap is equal to 0 or "Profile 31 - Use RnD parameters" was selected then 5G-L2-PS shall build selectedComponentTracesEvents based on R&D parameters:

rdComponentBitMapVector0 (See 5G_L2_16967_replaced([11188990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28MQjPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector1 (See 5G_L2_16971_replaced([11189001](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28MQjvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector2 (See 5G_L2_16975_replaced([11189013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3kfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector3 (See 5G_L2_16977_replaced([11189022](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3k_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector4 (See 5G_L2_16979_replaced([11189033](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3lfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector5 (See 5G_L2_16981_replaced([11189044](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3l_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapProfile (See 5G_L2_17107_replaced([11189055](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3mfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

otherwise 5G-L2-PS shall build selectedComponentTracesEvents based only on the parameter profileBitMap in trace start request L2LogDu_StreamActivationReq also including mandatory events (See 5G_L2_16986_replaced([11206915](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-NpzPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Note: Any updates of above R&D parameters after L2LogDu_StreamActivationReq and before L2LogDu_StreamDeactivationReq shall be internally disregarded if profile "Profile 31 - Use RnD parameters" was not selected.

After receiving request L2LogDu_StreamDeactivationReq:

5G-L2-PS shall update selectedComponentTracesEvents to keep all information up to date which events' type are active including mandatory and optional events and selected profiles based on current values of R&D parameters:

rdComponentBitMapVector0 (See 5G_L2_16967_replaced([11188990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28MQjPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector1 (See 5G_L2_16971_replaced([11189001](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28MQjvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector2 (See 5G_L2_16975_replaced([11189013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3kfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector3 (See 5G_L2_16977_replaced([11189022](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3k_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector4 (See 5G_L2_16979_replaced([11189033](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3lfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector5 (See 5G_L2_16981_replaced([11189044](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3l_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapProfile (See 5G_L2_17107_replaced([11189055](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3mfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[End CNI-107269]

5G-L2-PS before each writing attempt of Component Trace Event to buffer shall check the appropriate bit inside selectedComponentTracesEvents.

---

#### 2.12.2.3.0-2 Trace filtering (ID: 11206887)

**2.12.2.3.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `11206907`)

Trace filtering L2 trace shall support filtering on object level and on item level.

Filtering on object level:

Filtering object is cell (i.e. PCI) and it shall be identified by list of parameter physCellId in trace start request L2LogDu_StreamActivationReq.

5G-L2-PS shall support a list of up to 8 PCIs.

Filter object (i.e. PCI) list modification is not supported. In order to modify PCI list, the ongoing measurement must be stopped and new one started.

PCI filtering applies only to streaming traces.

Filtering on item level:

Trace items shall be activated / deactivated by R&D parameter that defines the filtering bitmap (See 5G_L2_16967_replaced([11188990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28MQjPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_L2_16971_replaced([11189001](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28MQjvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_L2_16975_replaced([11189013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3kfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_L2_16977_replaced([11189022](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3k_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_L2_16979_replaced([11189033](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3lfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_L2_16981_replaced([11189044](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3l_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

Each single bit from such bitmap should control only one Component Trace Event (also known as TTI Event).

Filtering R&D parameter is common to both stream and snapshot.

Filtering parameter shall be read at the startup and each time it is updated.

This feature introduces a new concept of mandatory and optional TTI Events. All events by default are optional except those listed here: 5G_L2_16986_replaced([11206915](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-NpzPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Mandatory events are always activated.

Optional events can be controlled by R&D parameters:

rdComponentBitMapVector0 (See 5G_L2_16967_replaced([11188990](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28MQjPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector1 (See 5G_L2_16971_replaced([11189001](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28MQjvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector2 (See 5G_L2_16975_replaced([11189013](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3kfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector3 (See 5G_L2_16977_replaced([11189022](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3k_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector4 (See 5G_L2_16979_replaced([11189033](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3lfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

rdComponentBitMapVector5 (See 5G_L2_16981_replaced([11189044](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3l_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

[CNI-107269]

This feature introduces a new concept of predefined profiles. Each Component Trace Event (TTI Event) can belong to one or many profiles. 5G-L2-PS shall support up to 32 different profiles.

The definition of each profile is described in 5G_L2_17109_replaced([11206928](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-NpzvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Profiles selection by R&D parameter: rdComponentBitMapProfile (See 5G_L2_17107_replaced([11189055](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28M3mfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or parameter profileBitMap in L2LogDu_StreamActivationReq.

[End CNI-107269]

After receiving notification from CC&S[CNI-107269], request L2LogDu_StreamActivationReq or L2LogDu_StreamDeactivationReq[End CNI-107269], 5G-L2-PS shall proceed as described in 5G_L2_17078_replaced([11206851](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-NpxfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In addition to the filtering bitmap, there is another filtering mechanism at item level, i.e. filtering due to decimation factor.

In the definition of each event, a decimation factor has to be defined. A decimation factor set to 1/N allows to collect the event only each N event occurences.

---

#### 2.12.2.3.0-3 Trace filtering List of mandatory events (ID: 11206915)

**2.12.2.3.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11206920`)

utcTimestampData (UTCTIMESTAMPDATA)

dlPreSchedData (DLPRESCHEDDATA)

dlFdSchedData (DLFDSCHEDDATA)

dlHarqRxDataArray (DLHARQRXDATAARRAY)

ulPreSchedData (ULPRESCHEDDATA)

ulFdSchedData (ULFDSCHEDDATA)

ulHarqRxData (ULHARQRXDATA)

Note: This is a special profile that shall be used only during streaming mode after receving L2LogDu_StreamActivationReq otherwise should be ignored.

---

### 2.12.2.4 Streaming mode

#### 2.12.2.4.0-2 Streaming mode L2 streaming traces are activated by the Logging Agent via the L2LogDu_StreamActivationReq message with traceType == ComponentTrace ** When activated, 5G-L2-PS shall add trace items into the stream message as long as there is free space and based on the filter R&amp;D parameter (see 5G_L2_1707_replaced( 11206842 )). This includes items from different users/features, carriers and TTIs. Stream messages shall not exceed standard Ethernet frame size (1500 bytes). 5G-L2-PS shall transmit stream message immediately when it gets full and shall not wait end of the TTI for sending. At the streaming trace stop, all stream messages not yet sent shall be discarded. Since DL/UL/[CB010680-I] Pos ( 11141055 ) [End CB010680-I] schedulers are on different cores, they shall send separate stream messages. (ID: 12516924)

### 2.12.2.5 Snapshot mode

#### 2.12.2.5.0-1 Snapshot mode 5G-L2-PS shall activate snapshot at the component start and it shall be active all the time. Items shall be written to snapshot based on the filter R&amp;D parameter (see 5G_L2_1707_replaced( 11206842 )). Snapshot shall behave in ring buffer manner so that most recent information is included and oldest items are discarded as file gets full. See 5G_L1L2_FLOW_696_replaced( 11204637 ) for snapshot buffer size. During Snapshot collection phase it is allowed to lose new trace data (i.e. the data that is generated during the collection). (ID: 11207483)

### 2.12.2.6 Parsing trace information

#### 2.12.2.6.0-1 Parsing trace information Parser tool for reading saved streaming trace files and snapshot files shall be implemented by L2 implementation team. Tool shall convert the trace data into human readable format. For streaming trace files, it is planned to use Wireshark tool and LUA decoding functions generated by MuLTI environment. For snapshot trace files, it is planned to convert binary files into .csv files readable via Excel. The conversion functions are generated by MuLTI environment as well. (ID: 11207497)

## 2.12.3 Snapshot documentation

### 2.12.3.0-1 Snapshot documentation Description Files (ID: 11207511)

**2.12.3.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11207532`)

Snapshot documentation For the all 5G-L2-PS component log files registered for the technical logs collection to be part of Single RAN BTS (SBTS) snapshot, 5G-L2-PS shall provide an entry in the trbl_log_list.csv and a respective description file. Name of the respective description file included to the package is defined in column ‘comment’ of the trbl_log_list.csv.

Following files are included by 5G-L2-PS:

|  |   
---|---|---  
File name | File container | File coverage level  
*_startup.log | *_startup.zip | Normal  
*_runtime.log | *_runtime.zip | Normal  
*_L2RtCpu_TimeMarkerSnapshot.txt | *_TimeMarkerSnapshot.txt.xz | [PR625553] Normal [End PR625553]  
*_L2TtiTrace_EventsSnapshot.bin |  | Normal  
  
For meaning of second column see further requirements.

---

## 2.12.4 L2PS trigger L1H capature antenna data

### 2.12.4.0-1 L2PS trigger L1H capature antenna data Purpose: Error scenarios during a call on L1 and / or L2 are difficult to capture. The errors occur for a very short duration and are very time critical, generally the error scenario is overwritten before manual intervention is done for log collection. In order to capture antenna data to analysis the error scenarios, abnormal events are used to trigger the automated IQ data capture when issue happens, the IQ data can be an input of data analyzer to reproduce the signal quality, CRC result, etc. For some complex scenarios, eg: radio link failure, TA errors and RACH process failure, L1 event cannot distinguish the errors, so we must depend on L2PS to trigger such events. Message: UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq send from L2-PS to L1H UL to trigger capture antenna data. Refer to 5G_L1_IF_14393_replaced( 11514696 ). Message: DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq send from L2-PS to L1H DL to trigger capture antenna data. Refer to 5G_L1_IF_14386_replaced( 11513201 ). (ID: 11207545)

### 2.12.4.1 Fast Antenna Snapshot Request

#### 2.12.4.1.0-1 Fast Antenna Snapshot Request EFS Sources: CNI-51355: https://collab.ext.net.nokia.com/ui#review:id=813572, CNI-51355 CP3 L2 U-Plane v2.pdf CNI-82417: https://collab.ext.net.nokia.com/ui#file:review=862543/promote=true/version=6319141, CNI-82417_L2-PS_v1.0.docx CNI-119513: https://jiradc.ext.net.nokia.com/browse/CNI-119513, CNI_119513_final-3.docx (ID: 11207557)

#### 2.12.4.1.1 Scope of implementation

##### 2.12.4.1.1.0-1 Scope of implementation (ID: 11207572)

**2.12.4.1.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11207585`)

Scope of implementation CNI-51355 CP2 defines an address exchange and a pair of FastAntennaSnapshot request/response messages for both UL and DL directions.

[CNI-82417-A] CNI-82417 extends the CNI-51355 functionality to capture L1 IQ Data in DL on L2 Abnormal events. CNI-51355 implements only UL direction, and with CNI-82417 DL direction will be supported and accordingly the required DL version of messages, triggers, abnormal events to be implemented. [End CNI-82417-A]

|   
---|---  
Message | First Feature  
UlData_AddressResp | CNI-51355  
UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq | CNI-51355  
UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp | CNI-51355  
DlData_AddressResp | CNI-82417  
DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq | CNI-82417  
DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp | CNI-82417

---

#### 2.12.4.1.2 Address exchange

##### 2.12.4.1.2.0-1 Address exchange (ID: 11207598)

**2.12.4.1.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11207605`)

Address exchange Dl/UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq

L2-PS shall use the EQID (Event Queue Id, 32 bits) address informed through fastAntennaSnapshotReqAddress IE in message Dl/UlData_AddressResp to send the message Dl/UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq to L1.

UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp (5G21A only)

As there is no counterpart EQID defined to send UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp to L2-PS, L1 shall reuse the same EQID of message PuschReceiveRespPs.

Dl/UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp (5G21B onwards)

L2-PS shall inform L1 the EQID in which it expects to receive in Dl/UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp through a new field in message Dl/UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq. This mechanism removes the dependency of this feature 3 Nokia internal use with PUSCH channel —critical for the implementation of DL— as PUSCH channel does not exist in particular situations where events might be triggered (for instance, RACH message 2)

---

#### 2.12.4.1.3 Trigger limitation

##### 2.12.4.1.3.0-1 Trigger limitation Trigger limitation for IQ data capture (ID: 11207618)

**2.12.4.1.3.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11207659`)

Trigger limitation In order to limit the number of requests triggered, L2-PS shall count the number of times a specific event occurred, independently of which UE triggered it. Hence, the limitation mechanism is global to all UEs, only restricted to the targeted subcell.

L2-PS shall stop sending requests for a particular event as soon as the count for that particular event reaches the value defined in rdAbnormalEventTriggerNumber, independently of which UE triggered it.. For instance, in this situation where rdAbnormalEventTriggerNumber=10:

|   
---|---  
Counter | Value  
RACH failure | 10  
RLF failure | 5  
TA error | 7  
  
RACH failure is blocked and shall not trigger any more requests as its count has reached the limit, whereas RLF failure may still trigger 5 more requests and TA error up to 3 more.

Additionally, as a prevention method to overflow communication with L1, L2-PS shall not send parallel requests to L1 and hence shall only send one concurrent request either for UL IQ capture or for DL IQ capture. L2-PS shall send the next request only after the previous response has been received. L2-PS shall drop all other requests until the awaited response arrives. Simultaneous UL & DL IQ capture is not supported, at a time only one can be enabled.

[image-0][image_desc]The image is a UML sequence diagram illustrating communication between two entities, L2PS and L1. The diagram depicts a series of messages exchanged over time, with L2PS on the left and L1 on the right. The sequence of events is as follows: 1\. **Event detection** occurs at L2PS. 2\. L2PS sends a message **1 UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq** to L1. This is represented by a blue arrow pointing from L2PS to L1. 3\. Another **Event detection** occurs at L2PS. 4\. L2PS sends a message **2 UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq (suppressed)** to L1. This message is marked with a red 'X' on the line, indicating it was suppressed or rejected. 5\. L1 sends a response message **3 UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp** back to L2PS. This is represented by a green arrow pointing from L1 to L2PS. 6\. A third **Event detection** occurs at L2PS. 7\. L2PS sends a message **4 UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq** to L1. This is another blue arrow. 8\. L1 sends a response message **5 UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp** back to L2PS, indicated by a green arrow. The diagram uses dashed lines to indicate the passage of time and the continuation of lifelines. The "Event detection" labels are hexagonal shapes with rounded corners, signifying a trigger or an event. The messages are labeled with numbers and descriptive names.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m24dng_5G_L2_Entity_Level_file_10841.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

[CNI-119513] Event counter shall not be increased if the Dl/UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq is suppressed or Dl/UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp with status is not equal to OK. [End CNI-119513]

---

#### 2.12.4.1.4 Counter reset

##### 2.12.4.1.4.0-1 Counter reset Counter reset to restart IQ data capture (ID: 11207672)

**2.12.4.1.4.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `12507838`)

Counter reset L2-PS shall **reset all event counters when cell reconfiguration message is received in any cell [PR825183] from the same L2-PS instance [End PR825183] . Cell reconfiguration is triggered from CP-CELL and propagated to all necessary components. To trigger this procedure successfully, operator must not change any service sensitive configuration in the cell but perform any minor configuration change that shall be propagated to L2-PS, such as link adaptation parameters, CA configuration or PDCCH attributes (among others).**

L2-PS shall reset **all event counters of UL / DL when rdAbnormalEventTriggerOnlineEnable is set to 1 (Enable L1 UL IQ capture) / 2 (Enable L1 DL IQ capture) each time. Note: rdAbnormalEventTriggerOnlineEnable being set to 1 (Enable L1 UL IQ capture) / 2 (Enable L1 DL IQ capture) means UL / DL IQ data collection triggered by L2PS events is enabled.**

Informatively, R&D parameter management tool may block the R&D parameter setting to the same value. If this were really the case, rdAbnormalEventTriggerOnlineEnable shall be set to 0 (Disable L1 IQ capture) firstly, then set to 1 (Enable L1 UL IQ capture) / 2 (Enable L1 DL IQ capture) again.

[image-0][image_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating the process of cell reconfiguration triggered by gNB-CU-CP in a 5G network. The diagram depicts interactions between different network functions: gNB-DU, 5G-CP-CELL, 5G-CP-NB, 5G-CP-JF, MPlane, and Timer. The diagram is divided into vertical lifelines representing each network function. Horizontal arrows indicate messages exchanged between these functions. The messages are numbered and include the message name and associated protocol data unit (PDU). The main flow of the diagram shows that the 5G-CP-CELL initiates cell reconfiguration under specific conditions: when CU-owned system information is modified, when the CELL BARRING status of a cell is modified, or when the list of available PLMNs is modified. The sequence of events is as follows: 1\. **Trigger for cell reconfiguration (5G_RAN_CP_22186)** : This message is sent from gNB-DU to 5G-CP-CELL, initiating the reconfiguration process. 2\. **Cplf_SendToDu (5G_RAN_CP_531)** : This message is sent from 5G-CP-CELL to 5G-CP-NB. 3\. **F1: GNB-CU CONFIGURATION UPDATE (5G_RAN_CP_496)** : This message is sent from 5G-CP-NB to gNB-DU. 4\. **F1: GNB-CU CONFIGURATION UPDATE ACKNOWLEDGE (5G_RAN_CP_499)** : This message is sent from gNB-DU to 5G-CP-NB, acknowledging the configuration update. 5\. **Cplf_ReceiveFromDu (5G_RAN_CP_532)** : This message is sent from 5G-CP-NB to 5G-CP-CELL. Additionally, there is an optional section labeled "(6) Opt" which details a scenario where the "CELL BARRING status of a cell is modified". This triggers the following messages: * **(5.1) CpNb_CellInfoUpdateRequired (5G_RAN_CP_12357439)** : Sent from 5G-CP-NB to 5G-CP-CELL. * **(6.2) CpNbCm_NetworkPlanUpdateInd (5G_RAN_CP_4036)** : Sent from 5G-CP-NB to 5G-CP-JF. * **(6.3) CpNb_CellInfoUpdateConfirm (5G_RAN_CP_12357440)** : Sent from 5G-CP-NB to 5G-CP-CELL. The diagram also shows timer interactions: * **Start timer1NonUeProcGuard** : This is initiated by MPlane. * **Stop timer1NonUeProcGuard** : This is also initiated by MPlane. The title of the diagram is "CPF-CU: Cell reconfiguration triggered by gNB-CU-CP".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m24dng_5G_L2_Entity_Level_file_10846.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

---

#### 2.12.4.1.5 UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq

##### 2.12.4.1.5.1 Event trigger

###### 2.12.4.1.5.1.0-1 Event trigger RLF (ID: 11207730)

**2.12.4.1.5.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11207741`)

Event trigger L2-PS triggers RLF (Radio Link Failure) in any of these situations:

  1. UE runs into DL out-of-sync with gNB as a consequence of DTX detection of CSI reports in PUCCH channels

  2. UE reaches maximum number of RACH attempts

  3. UE reaches maximum number of RLC retransmissions

  4. SCG change or SCG reconfiguration

  5. SRB3 integrity failure




CNI-51355 only triggers a snapshot request under the first situation (UE out-of-sync detected in CSI report). Specifically, in this situation gNB sends N310 consecutive "out-of-sync" indications to UE. In that moment UE starts T310 timer, after whose expiration RLF is triggered.

[image-0][image_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a "Radio Link Failure" scenario. A horizontal line represents time, with an arrow indicating the direction of time flow and labeled "t". On the left side of the diagram, above the time axis, there are multiple red downward-pointing arrows, indicating "N310 Out of Sync indications". These indications occur at the beginning of the time period shown. Below the time axis, an arrow points upwards to a specific point in time, labeled "T310 started". A light blue horizontal bar extends from this point to another point in time, where a similar upward-pointing arrow is labeled "T310 expired". This blue bar visually represents the duration of the T310 timer. To the right of the time axis, there is a dark blue rectangular box containing the text "RLF indication", signifying a Radio Link Failure indication that occurs after the T310 timer has expired.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m24dng_5G_L2_Entity_Level_file_10850.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

The default values of N310 and T310 are 10 and 2000ms respec

---

###### 2.12.4.1.5.1.0-2 Event trigger TA error (ID: 11207748)

**2.12.4.1.5.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11207756`)

Event trigger L2-PS shall trigger a request indicating TA (Time Alignment/Advance, depending on the sources) error in case two consecutive TA metrics differ greater than the RAD parameter rdPuschTaAbnormalThreshold. Specifically, L2-PS shall evaluate the field shortTermTaMetric contained in UePuschReceiveRespPs in two consecutive messages.

---

###### 2.12.4.1.5.1.0-3 Event trigger RACH failure (ID: 11207762)

**2.12.4.1.5.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11207769`)

Event trigger L2-PS shall trigger a request indicating RACH (Random Access Channel) failure in any of these three situations:

  1. Message 3 (PUSCH) reports CRC NOK

  2. Message 4 (PDCCH in NSA, PDxCH in non-NSA) reports HARQ NACK or DTX

  3. Message 5 (PUSCH) reports HARQ NOK or DTX. PUCCH HARQ has not been consi

---

###### 2.12.4.1.5.1.0-4 Event trigger UlHighBler (ID: 11207777)

**2.12.4.1.5.1.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11207783`)

Event trigger UlHighBler event is used to trigger UL IQ data capture after RACH.

Below R&D parameters should be correctly set when we need to trigger UL IQ data collection by L2PS event UlHighBler:

· Common parameters:

o rdAbnormalEventTriggerOnlineEnable is set to 1 (enable UL IQ data collection).

o rdAbnormalEventTriggerSubcellIdForIqCapture

o rdAbnormalEventTriggerNumber is set to a value greater than 1.

· Event specific parameters:

o rdAbnormalEventUlHighBlerEnable is set to 1 (Enable UlHighBler IQ capture during DCI 0_0 transient phase) or 2 (Enable UlHighBler IQ capture after RACH).

o rdUlHighBlerTxType

o rdThdNumOfNackDtxUlHighBlerEvent

o rdIsOnlyVoNRUeUlHighBlerEvent

rdAbnormalEventTriggerOnlineEnable need to be set to 1 (Enable L1 UL IQ capture) once again if we want to capture UL IQ data triggered by L2PS event once again. rdAbnormalEventTriggerOnlineEnable need to be set to 2 (Enable L1 DL IQ capture) once again if we want to capture DL IQ data triggered by L2PS event once again. See 5G_L2_19234_replaced([11207672](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-SiQfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for Counter reset to restart IQ data capture.

Note:

1) rdUlHighBlerTxType with value 0 (InitTx) means only PUSCH InitTx is considered, rdUlHighBlerTxType with value 1 (InitTx and ReTx) means both PUSCH InitTx and PUSCH ReTx are considered.

2) rdIsOnlyVoNRUeUlHighBlerEvent with value 1 (TRUE) means only VoNR UE is considered, otherwise, no limitation regarding UE type.

All below conditions should be met before trigger the UL IQ data collection request by L2PS event UlHighBler:

1) rdAbnormalEventUlHighBlerEnable is not set to 0 (Disable UlHighBler IQ capture after RACH)

2) event counter of UlHighBler does not reach rdAbnormalEventTriggerNumber. See 5G_L2_19232_replaced([11207618](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-R7QvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for event counter induced trigger limitation for IQ data capature.

3) if rdIsOnlyVoNRUeUlHighBlerEvent is set to 1 (TRUE), then only VoNR UE is considered, otherwise, then no limitation regarding UE type.

4) if rdAbnormalEventUlHighBlerEnable is set to 1 (Enable UlHighBler IQ capture during DCI 0_0 transient phase), then only PUSCH transmissions whose UL grant happens during DCI 0_0 transient phase are considered; if rdAbnormalEventUlHighBlerEnable is set to 2 (Enable UlHighBler IQ capture after RACH), then all PUSCH transmissions after RACH is considered.

5) if rdUlHighBlerTxType is set to 0 (InitTx), then only PUSCH InitTx is considered; if rdUlHighBlerTxType is set to 1 (InitTx and ReTx), then both PUSCH InitTx and PUSCH ReTx are considered

6) Number of consecutive PUSCH NACK/DTX of a UE who meets the conditions of 3), 4) and 5) is greater than rdThdNumOfNackDtxUlHighBlerEvent.

All BB boards (such as ABIL/ABIN/ABIO/ABIP/ASOE/etc) should have these changes.

---

##### 2.12.4.1.5.2 Message parameters (5G21A only)

###### 2.12.4.1.5.2.0-1 Message parameters (5G21A only) Message parameters (ID: 11207796)

**2.12.4.1.5.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11207802`)

Message parameters (5G21A only) The following table illustrates the message parameters sent to L1:

|   
---|---  
Parameter | Value or description  
numDlSubCellId | 0  
dlSubCellId | {0, 0, 0, 0}  
numUlSubCellId | 1  
ulSubCellId[0] | ulSubcellId in which the event occurred  
ulSubCellId[1..3] | 0  
sfn | SFN in which the event occurred  
slot | Slot in which the event occurred  
numOfEvents | 1  
eventsList[0] | {CRNTI, EventNb, EventType}  
eventList[0..7] | Uninitialized  
  
The range of values allowed for EventNb are the following ones:

|   
---|---  
EventNb | Value  
RadioLinkFailure | 50  
TimingAdvance | 51  
RachMsg3Failure | 52  
RachMsg4Failure | 53  
RachMsg5Failure | 54  
  
Finally, the range of values for EventType are:

|   
---|---  
EventType | Value  
PUSCH | 0  
PRACH | 1  
PUCCH | 2  
PUxCH | 3  
Common | 4

**2.12.4.1.5.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11207822`)

Message parameters (5G21B onwards) Branch 5G21B and subsequent

The following table illustrates the message parameters sent to L1:

|   
---|---  
Parameter | Value or description  
ulSubCellId | ulSubcellId in which the event occurred  
sfn | SFN in which the event occurred  
slot | Slot in which the event occurred  
numOfEvents | 1  
eventsList[0] | {CRNTI, EventNb, EventType}  
eventList[0..7] | Uninitialized  
  
The range of values of EventNb and EventType shall not suffer changes.

The range of values allowed for EventNb are the following one:

|   
---|---  
EventNb | Value  
RadioLinkFailure | 50  
TimingAdvance | 51  
RachMsg3Failure | 52  
RachMsg4Failure | 53  
RachMsg5Failure | 54  
  
Finally, the range of values for EventType are:

|   
---|---  
EventNb | Value  
PUSCH | 0  
PRACH | 1  
PUCCH | 2  
PUxCH | 3

##### 2.12.4.1.5.4 Trigger condition

###### 2.12.4.1.5.4.0-1 Trigger condition Trigger Condition (ID: 11207837)

**2.12.4.1.5.4.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11207850`)

Trigger condition Once L2-PS detects that one of the trigger events has occurred, a snapshot request shall be sent immediately if and only if all these conditions are fulfilled:

  1. RAD parameter rdAbnormalEventTriggerOnlineEnable is enabled

  2. RAD parameter rdAbnormalEventTriggerSubcellIdForIqCapture is equal to the UL subcell Id in which the event occurred

  3. For a specific event type, it must fulfill:




a. RAD parameter rdAbnormalEventTriggerNumber is greater than the number of events already occurred for this particular event type

b. RAD parameter flag is enabled, namely:

i. rdAbnormalEventRlfEnabled for RLF

ii. rdAbnormalEventPuschTaAbnormalEnabled for TA

iii. rdAbnormalEventRachFailureEnabled for RACH failure

[CNI-119513] iv. rdAbnormalEventUlHighBlerEnable is set to a value other than 0 (Disable UlHighBler IQ capture after RACH) [End CNI-119513]

  1. Guard timer is not running

  2. There are no UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp messages pending to be rece

---

#### 2.12.4.1.6 UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp

##### 2.12.4.1.6.0-1 UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp Overview (ID: 11207864)

**2.12.4.1.6.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12731540`)

UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp In case the status of UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp indicates NoError, L2-PS shall start a timer with a fixed duration indicated by the RAD parameter rdAbnormalEventGuardTimerLength with a default duration of 120 seconds.

In addition, if rdEnableSynchronizedL1L2Log([12681463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_hg2SQFDjEfCeeMlEb49MrQ)) is true and DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp indicates NoError, L2-PS shall start a freezetimer (indicated by rdFreezeTimerLength([12682063](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_WuTCwlDwEfCeeMlEb49MrQ)) is triggered with a default duration of 120 seconds during the snapshot collection.

During this interval either Guard Timer or Freeze Timer is active, L2-PS shall not send any more requests of any type, even if that particular type has not reached yet the limit.

In case the status of UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp indicates that L1 service is busy [PR840468] or min time rejection [End PR840468], L2-PS shall start a timer with a fixed duration indicated by the RAD parameter rdAbnormalEventGuardTimerLength with a default duration of **120 seconds. During this interval, L2-PS shall** not send any more requests of **any type, even if that particular type has not reached yet the limit.** ****

In addition, L2-PS shall **not stop the guard timer in case it receives any additional UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp messages containing a positive status. After timer start up, all subsequent responses shall be dropped, independently of the status they contain.**

L2-PS shall start the timer exclusively only in case it receives STATUS_4_SERVICEBUSY **[PR840468]** or STATUS_7_MINTIMEREJECTION **[End PR840468]** as the error status. The rest of the status are considered configuration errors and do not represent a busy state.

[image-0][image_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating a communication protocol between L2PS, L1, and EmTimer. The diagram depicts a series of events and responses exchanged between these entities. Here's a breakdown of the interactions: 1\. **Event detection** occurs at L2PS. 2\. L2PS sends a `UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq` to L1. 3\. L1 responds with `UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp`. 4\. L2PS receives a response with status `NoError`. 5\. Another **Event detection** occurs at L2PS. 6\. L2PS sends a `UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq` to L1. 7\. L1 responds with `UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp`. 8\. L2PS receives a response with status `ServiceBusy`. A "Guard timer" is initiated: 9\. `FastAntennaSnapshotGuardTimer` starts up, involving EmTimer. 10\. `UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp` is sent, but it is marked with a red 'X', indicating it's discarded. 11\. A note explains that during timer execution, any response shall be discarded independently of the status. 12\. Another **Event detection** occurs at L2PS. 13\. L2PS sends a `UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq` to L1. This request is also marked with a red 'X', indicating it's disregarded. 14\. A note explains that during timer execution, any other event requests shall be disregarded. 15\. `FastAntennaSnapshotGuardTimer` expires. 16\. A final **Event detection** occurs at L2PS. 17\. L2PS sends a `UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq` to L1. 18\. L1 responds with `UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp`. The diagram also includes page number "9" at the bottom right.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m24dng_5G_L2_Entity_Level_file_10869.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

Note 1: Above requirements further re-phrased and aligned based on to align current software implementation and along with RD Parameters rdEnableSynchronizedL1L2Log and rdFreezeTimerLength has been updated.

Note 2: Above call sequence has not been considered rdEnableSynchronizedL1L2Log execution sequence.

---

#### 2.12.4.1.7 DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq

##### 2.12.4.1.7.1 Event trigger

###### 2.12.4.1.7.1.0-1 Event trigger RLF (ID: 11207893)

**2.12.4.1.7.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11207910`)

Event trigger L2-PS triggers RLF (Radio Link Failure) in any of these situations:

1\. UE runs into DL out-of-sync with gNB as a consequence of DTX detection of CSI reports in PUCCH channels

2\. UE reaches maximum number of RACH attempts

3\. UE reaches maximum number of RLC retransmissions

4\. SCG change or SCG reconfiguration

5\. SRB3 integrity failure

CNI-81417 triggers a fastAntennaSnapshotReq under the situation

1\. UE out-of-sync detected in CSI report- Specifically, in this situation gNB sends N310 consecutive "out-of-sync" indications to UE. In that moment UE starts T310 timer, after whose expiration RLF is triggered.

[image-0][image_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a "Radio Link Failure" scenario. The diagram uses a timeline labeled 't' along the horizontal axis. On the left side of the timeline, there are several red downward-pointing arrows, labeled "N310 Out of Sync indications". These arrows are clustered together, suggesting a series of out-of-sync events. At a certain point on the timeline, a blue line segment begins, marked with an upward-pointing arrow and the label "T310 started". This blue line segment extends horizontally along the timeline. Further along the timeline, the blue line segment ends with a downward-pointing arrow and the label "T310 expired". Above and to the right of the "T310 expired" marker, there is a dark blue rectangular box containing the text "RLF indication". The overall diagram depicts a sequence of events related to radio link failure, where out-of-sync indications precede the start and expiration of a timer (T310), culminating in an RLF indication.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m24dng_5G_L2_Entity_Level_file_10873.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

The default values of N310 and T310 are 10 and 2000ms respectively.

2\. Max PDSCH retransmission – RLF_DL_HARQ

The rdRlpDetDlHarq threshold is reached, and L2-PS will send the RadioLinkStatusInd with status rlf=RLF_DL_HARQ to CP-RT, where tRLFindForDU is the timer is started in DU when RLF status message is sent from Packet Scheduler. When it expires, DU sends RLF indication to CU.

DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq shall be triggered when RLF_DL_HARQ is detected.

[image-1][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to Radio Link Failure (RLF) triggering and recovery in a communication system. The flowchart is organized into several sections, including a header, two main processing blocks (L2-PS and CP-RT), and input/output elements. The header section contains two columns: "RLF TRIGGER" and "RLF RECOVERY". Under each of these, there are rows indicating "HARQ" and "RLF HARQ detection Threshold" / "RLF HARQ recovery Threshold" with corresponding values "35" and "2". There is also a "Case 1" label. The main processing blocks are "L2-PS" and "CP-RT". From "L2-PS", there are two input paths: 1\. A blue box labeled "36 pucchReceiveReqHarqD with dtx = 1". 2\. A blue box labeled "3 pucchReceiveReqHarqD with dtx = 0". Both of these inputs lead to the "L2-PS" block, from which two output signals are generated: 1\. A red box labeled "RadioLinkStatusInd rifStatus = RLF_DL_HARQ". 2\. A green box labeled "RadioLinkStatusInd rifStatus = RLF_OFF". The "CP-RT" block receives signals from the outputs of "L2-PS". The red output from "L2-PS" leads to a decision point represented by a symbol that looks like a valve or a gate, which then leads to an output labeled "tRLFindForDU". The green output from "L2-PS" leads to a cross symbol, indicating a termination or rejection. The table representation of the header section is as follows:

Case | RLF TRIGGER | RLF RECOVERY  
---|---|---  
Case 1 | HARQ | HARQ  
| RLF HARQ detection Threshold | RLF HARQ recovery Threshold  
| 35 | 2

---

###### 2.12.4.1.7.1.0-2 Event trigger CQI (ID: 11207917)

**2.12.4.1.7.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11207932`)

Event trigger If CQI reported by UE:

  1. Below minimum threshold in consecutive report



 CQI monitoring if rdAbnormalEventCqiMonitoringEnabled=1 (enabled):

 Monitoring level Per UE

 Minimum CQI threshold defined via RAD param rdAbnormalEventCqiMinThreshold

 If CQI, is below threshold consistently for rdMaxConsecutiveLowCqiBeforeIQTrigger, trigger DL IQ capture

 The counter shall reset if any of the report have higher CQI than threshold i.e. CQI improves

 eventNb sent to L1 will be CQI monitoring

 L2PS will stop sending request for this event if trigger count is > rdAbnormalEventTriggerNumber  
  
---  
CQI Monitoring to trigger DL IQ capture (rdAbnormalEventCqiMonitoringEnabled=1 && maxTrigger<=10) If(currentCqi < rdAbnormalEventCqiMinThreshold) { If(belowThresholdCqiCounter == rdMaxConsecutiveLowCqiBeforeIQTrigger) { Trigger DL IQ capture maxTriggerCount++ } Else belowThresholdCqiCounter++; } Else { //reset counter belowThresholdCqiCounter = 0; } * rdMaxConsecutiveLowCqiBeforeIQTrigger = 5, i.e. after 5 consecutive report trigger DL IQ capture  
  
.[image-0][image_desc]The image contains two plots. The top plot is titled "csiSrReportData.csiReport.cqi". It shows a blue line graph with data points labeled with numbers. The x-axis is labeled with "sfn" and numbers ranging from 395 to 397. The y-axis is labeled from 0 to 12. A vertical yellow line is present, with text "Trigger DL IQ capture" to its right. The bottom plot is titled "csiReport.cqi - rdAbnormalEventCqiMinThreshold". It shows two line graphs: a blue line labeled "csiSrReportData.csiReport.cqi" and an orange line labeled "rdAbnormalEventCqiMinThreshold". The x-axis is labeled with numbers from 1 to 15. The y-axis is labeled from 0 to 12. A vertical yellow line is present at the x-axis value of 12. The overall image is a dark-themed graph with white and yellow text and lines.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m24dng_5G_L2_Entity_Level_file_10875.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

  1. Abnormal CQI jump



1\. jump if rdAbnormalEventCqiJumpEnabled=1(enabled)

a) Monitoring level Per UE

b) If cqi difference between previous and current is more than rdAbnormalEventCqiJumpStep, trigger DL IQ capture

c) eventNb sent to L1 will be CQI Jump

d) L2PS will stop sending request for this event if trigger count is > 10  
  
---  
CQI Jump to trigger DL IQ capture (rdAbnormalEventCqiMonitoringEnabled=1 && maxTriggerCount<=10) cqiDifference = (previousCqi > currentCqi? previousCqi- currentCqi: currentCqi- previousCqi) If(cqiDifference >= rdAbnormalEventCqiJumpStep) { Trigger DL IQ capture; maxTriggerCount++; }  
  
Example:

\- rdAbnormalEventCqiJumpStep = 7

\- The jump (in red) depict the abnormal CQI jump

\- [image-1][image_desc]The image is a line graph with a dark gray background and a grid. The x-axis represents time, labeled with "sfn" and numbers like "395", "396", and "397". The y-axis represents values from 0 to 16. A blue line plots data points, with numerical labels above each point. Red lines highlight specific segments of the blue line. Two vertical yellow lines are also present, marking specific points on the graph. The title of the graph is "csiReport.cqi (with rdAbnormalEventCqiJumpStep=7)". The data points on the blue line are: \- At "sfn", the value is 7. \- At "395", the value is 7. \- At "395", the value is 10. \- At "396", the value is 9. \- At "396", the value is 6. \- At "396", the value is 7. \- At "396", the value is 15. \- At "396", the value is 12. \- At "397", the value is 11. \- At "397", the value is 13. \- At "397", the value is 4. \- At "397", the value is 2. \- At "397", the value is 3. \- At "397", the value is 14. The red lines connect the following points: \- From the point with value 7 (at "396") to the point with value 15 (at "396"). \- From the point with value 13 (at "397") to the point with value 4 (at "397"). \- From the point with value 3 (at "397") to the point with value 14 (at "397"). The yellow lines are positioned at the x-axis labels "396" (between the points with values 15 and 12) and "397" (between the points with values 4 and 2). Below the graph, there is text that reads: "Trigger DL IQ capture on abnormal CQI jump".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m24dng_5G_L2_Entity_Level_file_10875.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]
