###### 2.12.4.1.7.1.0-3 Event trigger RACH failure (ID: 11207940)

**2.12.4.1.7.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11207953`)

Event trigger L2-PS shall trigger a request indicating RACH (Random Access Channel) failure in any of these [Before CNI-129090] three [End CNI-129090] situations:

 Message 3 (PUSCH) reports CRC NOK

 Message 4 (PDCCH in NSA, PDxCH in non-NSA) reports HARQ NACK or DTX

 Message 5 (PUSCH) reports HARQ CRC NOK or DTX. PUCCH HARQ has not been considered

 Message 3 (PUSCH) DTX

 [CNI-129090] Message 2 (PDSCH) failure followed by Message 3 (PUSCH) DTX [End CNI-129090]

---

##### 2.12.4.1.7.2 Message parameters

###### 2.12.4.1.7.2.0-1 Message parameters (ID: 11207968)

**2.12.4.1.7.2.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12519992`)

Message parameters The following table illustrates the message parameters sent to L1:

|   
---|---  
Parameter | Value or description  
addrDlFastAntennaSnapshotResp | Address to which L1 will dispatch the message  
dlSubCellId | For all abnormal events trigger, First subcell on the PCI [CB009543-A] For all abnormal events triggered for FDD supercell, subcell ID of the "Primary DL subcell" is filled [End CB009543-A]  
dlSubCellId_sfn_padding |   
sfn | SFN in which the event occurred  
slot | Slot in which the event occurred  
numOfEvents | 1  
numOfEvents_eventsList_padding |   
eventsList[0] | {CRNTI, EventNb, EventType}  
eventList[1..7] | Uninitialized  
  
The range of values allowed for EventNb are the following ones:

In addition to **EventNb defined in** CNI-51355 , Three new **EventNb have been introduced for Msg3Dtx, Cqi monitoring and CQI jump and RachMsg2Failure:** ****

|   
---|---  
EventNb | Value  
RadioLinkFailure | 50  
TimingAdvance | 51  
RachMsg3Failure | 52  
RachMsg4Failure | 53  
RachMsg5Failure | 54  
L2ExternalSnapshotRequest | 55  
DlBler | 56  
RankFailure | 57  
RtDftSrsPingpong | 58  
UlHighBler | 59  
RachMsg3DtxFailure | 60  
CqiAbnormalJump | 61  
CqiLowMonitoring | 62  
RachMsg2Failure | 63  
  
Finally, the range of values for **EventType are listed below. EventType PDSCH, PDCCH & CSI are introduced with this CNI:**

|   
---|---  
EventType | Value  
PUSCH | 0  
PRACH | 1  
PUCCH | 2  
PUxCH | 3  
Common | 4  
PDSCH | 5  
PDCCH | 6  
CSI | 7

---

##### 2.12.4.1.7.3 Trigger Condition

###### 2.12.4.1.7.3.0-1 Trigger Condition (ID: 11207997)

**2.12.4.1.7.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11208006`)

Trigger Condition Once L2-PS detects that one of the trigger events has occurred, a DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq() shall be sent immediately if and only if all these conditions are fulfilled:

1\. RAD parameter rdAbnormalEventTriggerOnlineEnable to be set as below for L2 triggered IQ capture using Dl/UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq:

a. rdAbnormalEventTriggerOnlineEnable = 0 : Disable L1 IQ capture

b. rdAbnormalEventTriggerOnlineEnable = 1 : Enable L1 UL IQ capture

c. rdAbnormalEventTriggerOnlineEnable = 2 : Enable L1 DL IQ capture

2\. For a specific event type, it must fulfill:

a. RAD parameter rdAbnormalEventTriggerNumber is greater than the number of events already occurred for this particular event type

b. RAD parameter flag is enabled, namely:

i. rdAbnormalEventRlfEnabled for RLF

ii. rdAbnormalEventRachFailureEnabled for RACH

iii. rdAbnormalEventCqiMonitoringEnabled for CQI monitoring

iv. rdAbnormalEventCqiJumpEnabled for CQI Jump

3\. Guard timer is not running

4\. There are no DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp messages pending to be received  
  
---  
NOTE: - Either UL IQ capture (i.e. UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq()/Resp()) or DL IQ capture can be enabled at a time, this is controlled by RAD param rdAbnormalEventTriggerOnlineEnable (0-default,1,2). At a time both DL and UL IQ capture cannot happen - dlSubcellId in DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq() shall be first subcell of the PCI.

---

###### 2.12.4.1.7.3.0-2 Trigger Condition Trigger Condition: RACH MSG2 Failure Detection (ID: 11208015)

**2.12.4.1.7.3.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11208028`)

Trigger Condition RACH MSG2 Failure Detection Flow

---

**2.12.4.1.7.3.0-2.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11208041`)

Trigger Condition Automatic detection of RACH MSG2 Failure and auto trigger L1 IQ Samples and L2 Component Traces.

[image-0][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating a process related to random access preamble reception and subsequent message handling in a communication system. The flowchart uses standard shapes: rounded rectangles for start/end points or processes, diamonds for decisions, and parallelograms for input/output or data storage. Arrows indicate the flow of control. Here's a breakdown of the flowchart's elements: 1\. **Start/Input:** The process begins with a rounded rectangle labeled "Random Access preamble received in UIData_PrachReceiveind". 2\. **Decision 1:** A diamond shape asks "nrCellld, beamld, featureFlag, initialTa criteria are met and diff in slots between previous and current MSG1 I= 0". * If "No", the flow returns to the start. * If "Yes", the flow proceeds. 3\. **Decision 2:** Another diamond shape asks "diff in slots between previous and current MSG1 == rdAbnormalEventRachMsg2FailureMonitoringPeriodicity". * If "Yes", the flow proceeds to "Store information: UE id - RNTI, previous xsfn = xsfn of current MSG1". * If "No", the flow proceeds to a parallelogram labeled "MSG2_potential_failure_detections = 0". 4\. **Process/Data Storage:** A parallelogram labeled "Store information: UE id - RNTI, previous xsfn = xsfn of current MSG1" stores relevant information. 5\. **Process:** A parallelogram labeled "MSG2_potential_failure_detections = 0" resets a counter. 6\. **Input/Process:** A parallelogram labeled "Received HARQ feedback for MSG3" indicates receiving feedback. 7\. **Decision 3:** A diamond shape labeled "Check HARQ Status" has three outgoing paths: * "ACK/NACK": Leads to "MSG2_potential_failure_detections = 0". * "DTX": Leads to a diamond shape asking "Is initial transmission". * The "No" path from "Is initial transmission" leads to a parallelogram labeled "MSG2_potential_failure_detections = 0". 8\. **Decision 4:** The diamond shape "Is initial transmission" has two outgoing paths: * "Yes": Leads to a parallelogram labeled "MSG2_potential_failure_detections++". * "No": Leads to a parallelogram labeled "MSG2_potential_failure_detections = 0". 9\. **Process:** A parallelogram labeled "MSG2_potential_failure_detections++" increments a counter. 10\. **Decision 5:** A diamond shape labeled "check MSG2_potential_failure_detections" has two outgoing paths based on a threshold comparison: * "< Detections_log_trigger_threshold": Leads to the "check MSG2_potential_failure_detections" diamond. * ">= Detections_log_trigger_threshold": Leads to a rounded rectangle labeled "Trigger FAS". 11\. **End:** The process concludes with the rounded rectangle "Trigger FAS". The flowchart outlines a logic for detecting potential failures in random access message exchanges, specifically MSG1 and MSG2, and triggering an action ("Trigger FAS") when a certain threshold of failures is reached. It also considers HARQ feedback and initial transmission status.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m24dng_5G_L2_Entity_Level_file_10889.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

File: CNI-129090.jpeg

Folder: [https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:f:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.11%20Traceability/2.11.4%20L2PS%20trigger%20L1H%20capature%20antenna%20data/2.11.4.1%20Fast%20Antenna%20Snapshot%20Request/2.11.4.1.7%20DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq?csf=1&web=1&e=yk2c6p](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:f:/r/sites/5Gdoc/AS_Tech_Spec/Drawings%20and%20diagrams%20for%205G/User%20Plane/5G%20L2%20Entity%20Level/2%205G-L2-PS%20Component%20Requirements/2.11%20Traceability/2.11.4%20L2PS%20trigger%20L1H%20capature%20antenna%20data/2.11.4.1%20Fast%20Antenna%20Snapshot%20Request/2.11.4.1.7%20DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq?csf=1&web=1&e=yk2c6p)

#### 2.12.4.1.8 DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp

##### 2.12.4.1.8.0-1 DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp Overview (ID: 11208055)

**2.12.4.1.8.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `12731545`)

DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp In case the status of DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp indicates NoError, L2-PS shall start a timer with a fixed duration indicated by the RAD parameter rdAbnormalEventGuardTimerLength with a default duration of **120 seconds.**

In addition, if rdEnableSynchronizedL1L2Log([12681463](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_hg2SQFDjEfCeeMlEb49MrQ)) is true and DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp indicates NoError, L2-PS shall start a freezetimer (indicated by rdFreezeTimerLength([12682063](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_WuTCwlDwEfCeeMlEb49MrQ)) is triggered with a default duration of 120 seconds during the snapshot collection.

During this interval either Guard Timer or Freeze Timer is active, L2-PS shall **not send any more requests of** any type, even if that particular type has not reached yet the limit.****

In case the status of DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp indicates that L1 service is busy **[PR840468]** or min time rejection **[End PR840468]** , L2-PS shall start a timer with a fixed duration indicated by the RAD parameter rdAbnormalEventGuardTimerLength with a default duration of **120** seconds. During this interval, L2-PS shall **not send any more requests of** any type, even if that particular type has not reached yet the limit.**_*_** *

In addition, L2-PS shall **not stop the guard timer in case it receives any additional DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotResp messages containing a positive status. After timer start up, all subsequent responses shall be dropped, independently of the status they contain.**

L2-PS shall start the timer exclusively only in case it receives STATUS_4_SERVICEBUSY **[PR840468]** or STATUS_7_MINTIMEREJECTION **[End PR840468]** as the error status. The rest of the status are considered configuration errors and do not represent a busy state.

[image-0][image_desc]The image is a sequence diagram illustrating a communication protocol between L1, L2-PS, and EmTimer. The diagram depicts various messages exchanged between these entities, along with timer events and conditional logic. Here's a breakdown of the elements: **Participants:** * **L1:** Likely represents Layer 1 or a lower-level component. * **L2-PS:** Likely represents Layer 2 - Protocol Stack or a similar entity. * **EmTimer:** Represents an entity related to timers, possibly an Event Manager Timer. **Messages and Events:** 1\. **Event Detection:** This is a recurring event, indicated by yellow oval shapes, signifying the detection of some event. 2\. **DlData_FastAntennaSnapReq:** This message is sent from L2-PS to L1. * The first instance is followed by a green arrow indicating a successful response. * The second instance is followed by a red arrow indicating a "Status ServiceBusy" response. * A later instance is sent from L1 to L2-PS. 3\. **DlData_FastAntennaSnapResp (Status OK):** This is a successful response message sent from L1 to L2-PS. 4\. **DlData_FastAntennaSnapResp (Status ServiceBusy):** This is a response message indicating a busy status, sent from L1 to L2-PS. 5\. **FastAntennaSnapshotGaurdTimer: Start up:** This indicates the initiation of a guard timer. 6\. **During timer execution any response shall be discarded independently of the status:** This is a textual annotation associated with the guard timer, explaining its behavior. 7\. **DlData_FastAntennaSnapResp():** This message is sent from L2-PS to L1. 8\. **Event Detection:** Another instance of event detection. 9\. **DlData_FastAntennaSnapReq():** This message is sent from L2-PS to L1. 10\. **During timer execution any other event request shall disregarded:** This is another textual annotation related to the guard timer's behavior. 11\. **FastAntennaSnpashotGuardTimer: Expiration:** This indicates the expiration of the guard timer. 12\. **Event Detection:** A final instance of event detection. **Conditional Logic/Timers:** * The diagram shows a dashed box labeled "Gaurd Timer" encompassing a section of the interactions, suggesting that the events and messages within this box are subject to the guard timer's control. * The "FastAntennaSnapshotGaurdTimer" is explicitly shown starting up and expiring. * The annotations clearly define how responses and requests are handled during the timer's execution. **Overall Flow:** The diagram appears to describe a scenario where L2-PS requests fast antenna snapshots from L1. Initially, the requests are successful. However, at some point, L1 becomes busy, and subsequent requests are met with a "ServiceBusy" status. A guard timer is introduced to manage these requests, ensuring that responses are discarded and other event requests are disregarded while the timer is active. Once the timer expires, the communication flow seems to return to a state where successful responses are possible.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m24dng_5G_L2_Entity_Level_file_10892.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]

Note 1: Above requirements further re-phrased and aligned based on to align current software implementation and along with RD Parameters rdEnableSynchronizedL1L2Log and rdFreezeTimerLength has been updated. 

Note 2: Above call sequence has not been considered rdEnableSynchronizedL1L2Log execution sequence.

### 2.12.4.2 Trigger L1H UL to capture antenna data

#### 2.12.4.2.1 PUSCH channel errors trigger

##### 2.12.4.2.1.0-1 PUSCH channel errors trigger UL High BLER related with RRC reconfiguration transient phase (ID: 11208084)

**2.12.4.2.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11208090`)

PUSCH channel errors trigger When UL IQ data capature trigger condition is satisfied for UL High BLER after RACH (see 5G_L2_19443_replaced([11207777](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-SiTfvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for detail), L2-PS shall trigger L1H capature antenna data for further analysis via UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq.

'eventNb' in msg should fill '59: UlHighBler';

'eventType' in msg should fill '0: PUSCH'.

---

#### 2.12.4.2.2 PUCCH channel errors trigger

#### 2.12.4.2.3 PRACH channel errors trigger

#### 2.12.4.2.4 SRS physical signal errors trigger

##### 2.12.4.2.4.0-1 SRS physical signal errors trigger WB SRS decoded RT beam errors (ID: 11208117)

**2.12.4.2.4.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11208133`)

SRS physical signal errors trigger DL BLER exceed threshold when PDSCH use RT beam for NewTx

---

**2.12.4.2.4.0-1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11208139`)

SRS physical signal errors trigger DL BLER(only statistics PDSCH HARQ NACK when use RT beam in NewTx, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_21376_replaced([11157453](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ks7gN_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) ratio is a critical criterion to check whether RT beam is correct reflect the channel status. If WB SRS UE DL BLER ratio greater than threshold (target bler + rdDeltaBlerUsingRtBeam:5G_L2_15388_replaced([11189194](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28OFtPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), and rdRtBeamAbnormalTriggerBitMap(reference to 5G_L2_15348_replaced([11189184](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28OFsvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) bit 0 is set 1, L2-PS need to trigger L1H capature antenna data for further analysis.

'eventNb' in msg should fill '56: DL BLER';

'eventType' in msg should fill '4:SRS'.

---

**2.12.4.2.4.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11208145`)

SRS physical signal errors trigger Good CQI with bad rank when UE configured with 4 ports UE specific CSI-RS

---

**2.12.4.2.4.0-1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11208153`)

SRS physical signal errors trigger If UE configured with WB SRS, and 4 ports UE specific CSI-RS, CSI-RS will also send with RT beam. If CSI-RS RT beam is not correct or correlation is not good, it will impact UE feedback RI value. So if UE reported with good CQI value (>10), but rank < 3, it is possible there is some problem with CSI-RS RT beam, if rdRtBeamAbnormalTriggerBitMap(reference to 5G_L2_15348_replaced([11189184](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28OFsvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) bit 1 is set to 1, L2-PS need to trigger L1H capature antenna data for further analysis.

'eventNb' in msg should fill '57: Rank Failure';

'eventType' in msg should fill '4:SRS'.

---

**2.12.4.2.4.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `11208160`)

SRS physical signal errors trigger WB-SB SRS ping-pong switch

---

**2.12.4.2.4.0-1.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11208165`)

SRS physical signal errors trigger UE frequent switch between WB SRS and SB SRS is also not expect, which means UE works in WB SRS is not stable, it may related to RT beam not reflect current channel status. Following phenomenon will consider as exception case:

If in a monitor period: WB switch to SB SRS prohibit timer * 3 + SB swith to WB SRS prohibit timer * 3 + 200 (signaling transmit cost) * 6, there are equal or moren than three times of switch happen, it will be taken into account as ping-pong switch case. if rdRtBeamAbnormalTriggerBitMap(reference to 5G_L2_15348_replaced([11189184](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_28OFsvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) bit 2 is set to 1, L2-PS need to trigger L1H capature antenna data for further analysis.

There are several reason will lead to UE switch from WB SRS to SB SRS, to simply implementation, all reason lead to switch will be taken into account.

'eventNb' in msg should fill '58: RT-SRS/DFT-SRS ping-pong';

'eventType' in msg should fill '4:SRS'.

## 2.12.5 L1-L2 EM SW events tracing

### 2.12.5-1 L1 side tracing

#### 2.12.5-1.0-1 L1 side tracing When rdNumConsecutiveInvalidFakeUeMeas (see 12747794) is greater than zero, 5G-L2-PS shall continuously monitor the received measurements for scheduled Fake UEs for their validity. The purpose is to detect the abnormal scenarios where measurements data received from L1 for Fake UE is invalid for a prolonged time. Continuous invalid measurements for fake UEs implicates there is general issue in L1/RF side on the PUSCH channel. 5G-L2-PS shall follow dedicated algorithm: State variables per subcell: •    numOfConsecInvalidFakeUeMeas: the number of consecutive invalid fake UE measurements •    currentAntIndex: the index of current antenna stream monitored (up to max antenna streams) When 5G-L2-PS receives UlData_PuschReceiveRespPs (see 5G_L1_IF_200_replaced(11513831)), for each subcell identified and for every grant with the fakeUe IE set to true: 5G-L2-PS shall get the PuschAntMeas measurements by currentAntIndex. If the linRssiOfAnt or linNoiseOfAnt are invalid, then 5G-L2-PS shall increase the numOfConsecInvalidFakeUeMeas by one. Otherwise 5G-L2-PS shall set numOfConsecInvalidFakeUeMeas to zero and set currentAntIndex to the next antenna. When numOfConsecInvalidFakeUeMeas reaches the rdNumConsecutiveInvalidFakeUeMeas then 5G-L2-PS shall reset it to zero, log syslog warning with available debug information and suspend monitoring for 100 SFNs to avoid log flooding. Note: See 11513842: PuschAntMeas: for linRssiOfAnt, linNoiseOfAnt parameters and definitions of their invalid values. (ID: 12747757)

### 2.12.5.1 L1 side tracing

#### 2.12.5.1.0-1 L1 side tracing Marking EM SW events to be traced by L1 (ID: 11208213)

**2.12.5.1.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `13481587`)

L1 side tracing [Before CNI-135812-A]  
If rdPhysCellIdToBeMarkedForEMtrace (see 5G_L2_23638_replaced([11208226](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-U-jvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is set to value in range 0-1007 then, for cells with physCellId in PsCell_CellSetupReq message (see 5G_L2_IF_61_replaced([10967196](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2UTTc_u6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ))) equal to rdPhysCellIdToBeMarkedForEMtrace value  
[CNI-135812-A]  
If L2LogDu_StreamActivationReq is received with traceType equal to L1LongDurationTrace (see [10994279](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2XDsjvu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) then for the NR cell identified by nrCellId[End CNI-135812-A], 5G-L2-PS shall mark cell level EM SW events as candidates to be captured (traced) by L1. These EM SW events are:

  * DlData_SlotTypeReq

  * DlData_PatternConfigReq

  * DlData_SsBlockSendReqL1sw

  * DlData_PdcchSendReqL1sw

  * DlData_PdschSendReqL1sw

  * DlData_CsiRsSendReqL1sw

  * DlData_RimRsSendReq

  * DlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq

  * UlData_PuschReceiveReq

  * UlData_PuschReceiveReqL1ru

  * UlData_PucchReceiveReqL1sw

  * UlData_PrachReceiveReq

  * UlData_SrsReceiveReq

  * UlData_SrsPosReceiveReq

  * UlData_RimReceiveReq

  * UlData_FastAntennaSnapshotReq




If EM SW event is a candidate to be traced by L1 then 5G-L2-PS shall set trace streaming bit in EM SW event header to 1.

Otherwise 5G-L2-PS shall set trace streaming bit to 0.

[CNI-135812-A]5G-L2-PS shall deactivate all of EM SW events tracing when it receives L2LogDu_StreamDeactivationReq with traceType equal to L1LongDurationTrace (see [10994318](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2XETlfu6Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)).  
[End CNI-135812-A]

For more information see BTSC_L1_Req_14799 and Transport layer Architecture Specification:

[https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/btscommsarchandinterfaces/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B244D2504-9814-4FDC-99B1-25F2538FCBCE%7D&file=Transport%20layer%20Architecture%20Specification.docx&action=default&mobileredirect=true&DefaultItemOpen=1](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/sites/btscommsarchandinterfaces/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B244D2504-9814-4FDC-99B1-25F2538FCBCE%7D&file=Transport%20layer%20Architecture%20Specification.docx&action=default&mobileredirect=true&DefaultItemOpen=1)

#### 2.12.5.1.0-2 L1 side tracing rdPhysCellIdToBeMarkedForEMtrace (ID: 11208226)

**2.12.5.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `13481593`)

L1 side tracing [Before CNI-135812-A]  
Short description: Phys cell id of cells for which EM SW events shall be marked as candidates to be captured (traced) by L1. 1008 is a special value used to disable functionality.

Value range: 0 ... 1008

Default value: 1008

Step size: 1

Unit: -

Online modifiable: Yes

Type: Debug

Performance Impact: None

Used in: 5G_L2_23637_replaced([11208213](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2-U-jPvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Trigger for deletion: When filtering by phys cell id is no longer needed.

Owner: Wojciech Musial (wojciech.musial@nokia.com)  
[End CNI-135812-A]
